title: Economists and AI Lab leaders converge as AI reshapes the job ladder - TCR 07/14/26
description:
More than 200 economists, 16 Nobel laureates, and leaders from OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and Anthropic signed an 88-word statement urging policymakers to act on AI and jobs, New York became the first state to freeze new environmental permits for hyperscale data centers over 50 megawatts, and Meta more than doubled its Louisiana Hyperion campus to 5 gigawatts and up to $50 billion.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-14-2026/

0:00 200 economists and lab leaders call to act on AI jobs
0:27 New York's first statewide data-center moratorium
4:09 New York Draws the First Line on Data-Center Demand
6:18 Meta's $50bn Hyperion buildout races local governance
9:00 Nadella warns proprietary-model users may pay twice
12:17 MIT grows blood vessels as a model reads disease early
15:12 Growing Blood Vessels by Shaking Them
18:33 Closing and Synthesis

This episode covers the "We Must Act Now" AI-jobs statement and Stanford's early-career displacement data, New York's first-in-the-nation data-center moratorium, Meta's $50 billion Hyperion expansion racing local governance, Satya Nadella's warning that proprietary-model users may "pay twice," Tracebit's "context bombing" defense that turns attacking agents' guardrails against them, and MIT's magnet-grown blood vessels alongside the ProLM disease-prediction model.

The day's signal is convergence: the economists who model markets and the labs building frontier systems agree on little, and Monday they agreed the AI surplus is arriving faster than the rules for sharing it. Stanford's Canaries Dashboard shows the pressure landing first on the bottom rung, with early-career workers in exposed fields down 2.7 percent even as developer postings climb 15 percent and demand senior experience. The same repricing runs through the buildout: New York paused hyperscale permits and opened a review of the tax breaks that subsidized these campuses, while Meta cemented a five-gigawatt Louisiana campus wired to seven new plants and six states passed binding cost-allocation rules. Underneath the friction, the transition assembles its own tools - defenders turned an attacking agent's safety training into a kill switch that cut full-system seizures from 57 percent to 5 percent, MIT grew branching blood vessels by jostling them with a magnet, and a plasma-protein model read 16 chronic diseases up to 15 years before diagnosis.
